mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equity cash flows, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is trending down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average during FEDFUNDS downtrends, or if the signal's walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, real rates are restrictive and policy is likely suppressing risk appetite; this should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates weakening labor demand and softer future earnings, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days after a clear unemployment upshift.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the trigger does not outperform a zero-signal benchmark in out-of-sample classification for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating PAYEMS growth with stable UNRATE signals broad payroll expansion and stronger nominal demand, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combinations of rising PAYEMS and flat-to-falling UNRATE fail to produce above-baseline 60-day returns, or if the interaction term loses significance in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS reflects improving consumer spending and revenue expectations, which should lift equity prices over the next 5 trading days as analysts revise sales assumptions upward.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon returns are not positive after RSAFS accelerates, or if the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from 50% over any 18-month rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in INDPRO indicates industrial output contraction and margin pressure in cyclical sectors, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days when production weakens materially.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 1% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not deliver lower 20-day returns than untriggered episodes, or if the signal fails to maintain a negative coefficient in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Weak HOUST combined with elevated UNRATE marks a demand contraction in housing and labor, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through lower cyclical earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average while UNRATE is at or above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-labor weakness condition does not predict below-median 60-day returns, or if the effect disappears when tested separately for each proxy.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-inflation disinflation regime, a falling CPIAUCSL paired with a flat FEDFUNDS rate raises real policy tightness less than expected and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling inflation with unchanged policy does not improve 20-day returns relative to baseline, or if the interaction term is insignificant in any period where CPIAUCSL trends down for at least 3 months.
